title: City Harvest Mobile Market – Melrose (Bronx)
tagline: Join us for a variety of free food!
description: Grab free bags of fresh fruit, seasonal vegetables, and pantry items at the City Harvest Mobile Market in Melrose. One bag per family; supplies are limited and distributed first-come, first-served. Suited for families with kids 0–12 — children can taste different fruits, pick items they like, and learn simple healthy-eating tips. Located at the Melrose Food Distribution Center in the Bronx.
description_source: Free distribution of fresh fruits, seasonal vegetables, and non-perishable food items. One bag per family. Supplies limited; first-come, first-served.

title: Unique Abilities 5K Run/Walk
tagline: Join us for a fun and meaningful run/walk!
description: Run or walk a 5K at Cooper’s Beach on May 2, 2026, supporting Preston’s Path Foundation and inclusion. Families and kids ages 5–18 will find water stations, food, music, community resource tables, free finish-line photos, T-shirts for early sign-ups and finisher medals. Runners get awards for top finishers; kids will enjoy the beach setting and a friendly, supportive race atmosphere. Entry is $33.80.
description_source: A community 5K run/walk hosted by Preston’s Path Foundation to promote inclusion and awareness. Takes place on May 2, 2026, at Cooper’s Beach, Southampton, NY. Includes water stations, food, music, community resource tables, free finish line photos, T-shirts for early registrants, and finisher medals. Top three male and female finishers receive awards. Event runs 9:30 AM–12:30 PM.

title: Friday Night Lights Everyone FREE B4 12am w/RSVP at Mama Taco Bk
tagline: Party free before midnight with RSVP!
description: Head to Mama Taco in Brooklyn for Friday Night Lights, a 21+ party with DJs SIR MANNY, DIVINE, and YUNG B spinning. Women get in free before midnight, men free before 11PM, and birthday guests free all night—RSVP removes the cover. Enjoy 2-for-1 drink specials before midnight and the kitchen open until 1AM.

title: Farmhouse Family Day: Waking up the Farm!
tagline: Celebrate Earth Day with nature-based art activities!
description: Drop by the Wyckoff House Museum in Brooklyn for Farmhouse Family Day — nature-based art activities run 11 AM–3 PM. Kids ages 4–12 can make crafts from leaves, twigs and other natural materials and join a family tour at 12:30 to explore the historic farm and learn about plants and animals. Free entry; a hands-on way for children to try everyday farm tasks.
description_source: Family-friendly event featuring nature-based art making activities and guided tours of the Wyckoff House Museum. Activities run from 11:00 AM to 3:00 PM. Family tour at 12:30 PM. Open to children aged 4–12.

title: Blooming Minds - Free Pizza Party by Safe Pathways (Brooklyn)
tagline: A mental health-based pizza party for youth
description: Free pizza and hands-on crafts for people aged 10–24 who want to talk about mental health in a low-key setting. Kids and teens will decorate a "growth mindset brain," try creative prompts, and practice simple coping and connection skills with peers. It’s a casual community meet-up in Brooklyn where snacks and conversation are the focus.
description_source: Free pizza party for youth aged 10-24, focused on mental health and resilience. Participants engage in arts and crafts activities to build a growth mindset, including decorating a 'growth mindset brain' to explore personal inspiration. Activities promote social connection and coping skills in a supportive environment.

title: NYC Spring Youth Flag Rugby at PS 111
tagline: Join Former USA Eagle Chris Mattina for a 14-session Spring Flag Rugby program!
description: Weekly 14-session spring flag rugby program at PS 111 led by former USA Eagle Chris Mattina. Kids 6–15 (no experience needed) learn passing, evasive moves and teamwork through drills, small-sided games and athletic-development exercises in a non-contact setting. The season finishes with a tournament on Randall’s Island. Prices: $550 full, $300 half, $50 drop-in.
description_source: 14-session youth flag rugby program for children aged 6 to 15, no experience required. Includes technical drills, small-sided games, athletic development exercises, and a final tournament at Randall’s Island. Non-contact, focused on building confidence, teamwork, and athletic skills.

title: The Story - Brandi Carlile | One-Day Choir
tagline: Join us for a joyful singing experience!
description: Sing Brandi Carlile’s "The Story" with a welcoming community choir at Farm To People in Brooklyn. All ages and abilities (about 5–80) join singing games, learn harmony parts from provided sheet music and recordings, and perform together — no auditions or judgment. Kids get to try different vocal lines and sing in a supportive group. No one turned away for lack of funds.
description_source: A community singing event where participants of all ages and skill levels rehearse and perform Brandi Carlile's 'The Story' through singing games and group harmony, with no auditions or judgment. Includes sheet music, vocal recordings, and a supportive group experience.

tagline: NYC's #1 Magic Show for All Ages

tagline: Discover music from around the world
description: Musical Explorers is a family concert at Zankel Hall featuring Sephardi Ladino, Iranian, and Chilean folk songs performed live by Lily Henley, Mehrnam Rastegari, and Claudia Acuña. title: New Owner
tagline: A puppetry journey of friendship and loss
description: A puppet-and-animatronics show about Bart, a dog who leaves home after his owner dies and navigates city dangers and friendships. Kids will see expressive puppets, simple animation, and a gentle story about loss, loyalty, and making new friends. Best for ages 7–12; tickets are $25 at the New Victory, with a puppetry demo after each performance.
description_source: Bart, a young dog adopted by Mabel to help heal her widowed heart, embarks on an urban odyssey after her death, navigating towering hot dog carts, dog-nappers, and junkyard camaraderie through tender puppetry and animation. The show includes a puppetry demonstration after each performance.

title: FAD Market: Mother’s Day
tagline: Celebrate Mother’s Day weekend with over 35 independent designers.

title: 20th Annual John May Mile & 5K Run/Walk
tagline: Benefit race for the Greenport Fire Department
description: Join the 20th annual John May Mile & 5K on May 9 at Peconic Landing in Greenport. Pick a 1‑mile walk or a 3.1‑mile run — great for families and kids (ages 5+) who want to try a short race. Afterward there’s a barbecue, raffles and age‑group awards, and proceeds support the Greenport Fire Department.
description_source: The 20th annual John May Mile & 5K Run/Walk takes place on May 9, 2026, at Peconic Landing in Greenport, NY. Participants can choose between a 1-mile walk or a 3.1-mile run. Registration begins at 7:30 a.m. at Brecknock Hall, with the event starting at 9 a.m. The event includes a barbecue lunch, raffles, and awards for top finishers in age groups. Proceeds benefit the Greenport Fire Department. The event is open to runners and walkers of all ages and fitness levels.

title: Oyster Bay Brewing Company & Matthew Fetzer Foundation 5k Run/Walk & Kids Fun Run
tagline: Join us for a fun run for a great cause!

title: Soulful Sounds in Rockland
tagline: A night of soulful fun and vintage glamour
description: A Jazzy R&B duo brings Harlem Renaissance–inspired songs, duets, playful comedy and freestyling to The Angel Nyack. Kids (ages 5+) can bop along, hear live improvisation and try on 1920s-style looks—there’s a best-dressed prize. Complimentary wine and snacks for adults; tickets run $40–$90.

The performance is based on live music and comedy, not theatrical reenactment.

title: Cliff Runner 5K
tagline: Experience a certified 5K run through the scenic village of Sea Cliff
description: This is a certified 5K run/walk through Sea Cliff, starting at the Firehouse and finishing on Sea Cliff Beach. Open to runners and walkers ages 8–99 with team options; entry is free (donations encouraged) and the first 250 registrants get a T-shirt and personalized bib plus finish-line photos. Kids can race with friends and soak up the finish-line buzz; there’s a post-race BBQ, live music and a shuttle from the yacht club.
description_source: Annual 5K run/walk organized by the Sea Cliff Fire Department. Certified 5 km course starts at the Sea Cliff Firehouse and ends along Sea Cliff Beach. Open to runners and walkers, with team options. First 250 registrants receive a T-shirt, personalized bib, live results, free finish line photos and video. Post-race BBQ and live music provided. Shuttle service available from Sea Cliff Yacht Club to starting line.

CHDs affect 1 in 110 babies in the U.S. Participants include families, survivors, and supporters.

title: 6th Annual J. Lew Memorial 5K Run/Walk
tagline: Join us for a race in memory of Joe Lewinger
description: Join the 6th Annual J. Lew Memorial 5K at The Mary Louis Academy — an in-person or virtual race honoring Joe Lewinger and supporting the school's girls' athletics. Kids (ages 5+) can try a real race with personalized bibs, finish-line photos and early T-shirts, and there are awards by age, faculty, students and alumni. In-person registration is $38.98; virtual $35.
description_source: The 6th Annual J. Lew Memorial 5K Run/Walk honors Joe Lewinger, a beloved faculty member of The Mary Louis Academy who served as a Social Studies teacher, Athletic Director, and Assistant Principal of Student Life until his passing in March 2020. The event supports the TMLA Athletics Department, the largest all-girls athletic program in New York City. Participants may register for in-person or virtual participation. The race includes personalized bibs, finish line photos, and T-shirts for early registrants. Awards are presented for top faculty, student, alumni, and age group finishers.
price_summary: In-person registration: $38.98; Virtual participation: $35; Processing fees included. Free registration available on tmla.org but requires registration via elitefeats.com.

title: Sensory Sensitive Morning: Children’s Zoo
tagline: A morning of sensory inclusive fun!
description: Spend a quieter morning at the Bronx Children’s Zoo exploring exhibits, trying hands-on activities, and meeting an animal up close before the crowds arrive. Member $36 / Non-member $45.

title: 2026 New York Lymphoma Walk
tagline: Join us for a family-friendly event supporting lymphoma research
description: Join the New York Lymphoma Walk at the Seaport (Pier 16/17), a free, family-friendly fundraiser that brings patients, survivors, care partners and supporters together. The program includes opening remarks, a 10:15 a.m. walk, a Family Fun Zone with games and giveaways, light refreshments and an awards ceremony. Kids (0–12) can play games, grab giveaways and see the community come together for lymphoma research.
description_source: The Lymphoma Research Foundation hosts its annual New York Lymphoma Walk at the South Street Seaport, Pier 16, to raise funds for lymphoma research and honor those affected by blood cancer. The event includes opening ceremonies at 9:45 a.m., a walk beginning at 10:15 a.m., a Family Fun Zone with games and giveaways, light refreshments, and an awards ceremony. Participants include patients, survivors, care partners, families, and supporters.

title: Footsteps for Progress 5K "Color Bash"
tagline: A vibrant 5K technicolor run through Flushing Meadows Corona Park!
description: Join the Footsteps for Progress Color Bash 5K at Flushing Meadows Corona Park — a family-friendly run/walk with bursts of washable color powder, music, snacks and awards. Kids 12 and under can run the free Kids Fun Run, race for a finisher medal and soak up the DJ energy. Registration fee applies; proceeds support programs for people with developmental disabilities.
description_source: Annual community 5K run and walk hosted by Queens Centers for Progress, featuring color powder bursts (non-toxic and washable), a free Kids Fun Run for children ages 12 and under, personalized race bibs, dri-fit shirts, finisher medals, snacks, drinks, DJ, and awards for top finishers. Supports programs for over 1,200 children and adults with developmental disabilities.
price_summary: Includes processing fee; Kids Fun Run is free.

title: Footsteps for Progress 5K Run/Walk & Kids Fun Run
tagline: A fun family event promoting community involvement
description: Run or walk the Footsteps for Progress 5K at Flushing Meadows Corona Park and bring kids for a free Fun Run (ages 5–12). Kids can try a short, energetic race, meet other children and enjoy finish-line snacks, drinks and awards. Registration starts at $40 (early bird); a virtual option is available.
description_source: Join the annual Footsteps for Progress 5K Run/Walk and free Kids Fun Run at Flushing Meadows Corona Park to support Queens Centers for Progress, which serves individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities. Registration begins at 8:00 AM, followed by the Kids Fun Run at 8:30 AM and the 5K at 9:00 AM. The event includes a finish line celebration with snacks, drinks, and awards. Rain or shine. Virtual option available.

description: Hands-on STEAM session at The Hampton Library for kids aged 8–10, held monthly. title: Family Matinee: Flamenco Vivo Carlota Santana
tagline: An evening-length work exploring the essence of flamenco
description: See Flamenco Vivo Carlota Santana’s QUINTO ELEMENTO at the Joyce Theater, a full-length flamenco performance with live music by Francis Gómez exploring the idea of "ether." Kids ages 5–15 will get to watch dramatic footwork, clap along to rhythms, and feel the music up close. Tickets $17–$72; there’s a post-show meet-and-greet with the artists.
description_source: Flamenco Vivo Carlota Santana presents the New York City premiere of QUINTO ELEMENTO (Fifth Element), an evening-length performance featuring traditional flamenco dance and live music by Francis Gómez, exploring the element of ether as a unifying force. Includes a post-show meet-and-greet with artists.
city_district: Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea

title: BAFFA’s Annual Juried High School Art Exhibit
tagline: An exhibition featuring talented high school students' artwork
description: See work by students from 13 area high schools at BAFFA’s juried high school art show, open Apr 25–26, 2026, 1–4pm at the BAFFA Art Gallery in Sayville. Kids and teens can explore different styles, get ideas for their own projects, and enjoy the awards ceremony on Apr 25, 2–3pm. Free admission.
description_source: BAFFA’s Annual Juried Invitational High School Art Show features artwork from students at thirteen area high schools. The exhibit is open to the public on April 25–26, 2026, from 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM at the BAFFA Art Gallery. An awards ceremony will be held on April 25 from 2:00 PM to 3:00 PM, judged by Diana Aliberti, Lisa Stanko, and Rick Wenner. Participating schools include Bayport-Blue Point, Bay Shore, Bellport, Brentwood, Centereach, Commack, Connetquot, Longwood, Newfield, Patchogue-Medford, Sachem East, Sachem North, and Sayville. Admission is free.

title: Royal Princess Tea Party
tagline: Dress up in your royal best!
description: Spend 90 minutes at Ballet Long Island in Ronkonkoma watching professional dancers play princesses, then enjoy a served tea with lemonade, cupcakes and cookies. Adults must accompany children; tickets are $25 and proceeds support the school’s scholarship and teacher funds.

title: Earth Day Barnyard Bash
tagline: A family-friendly day of learning, adventure, and hands-on nature fun!
description: Bring kids to the Earth Day Barnyard Bash at Tanger Outlets Deer Park for a day of hands-on nature activities. Children can peek into a rooftop hive and taste local honey in the Honeybee Workshop, meet gentle animals at the petting zoo, get nature-themed face paint, and try a scavenger hunt with small prizes. Suitable for ages 0–12; all activities are free.
description_source: Activities include a Honeybee Workshop with rooftop hive exploration, local honey tasting, and planting a pollinator-friendly flowerpot; a Petting Zoo with gentle farm animals and care education; nature-themed face painting; and a scavenger hunt for children 12 and under with limited prizes. All activities are free.

title: Super Family Saturday: Artful Earth
tagline: Celebrate Earth Day with creativity and nature
description: Bring kids to Super Family Saturday: Artful Earth at the Manes Art Education Center (Nassau County Museum of Art) on April 25, 1–4 PM — a free, hands-on Earth Day program. Best for ages 5–14 (children under 18 need an adult), families can make outdoor art, join nature and bird walks, encounter wildlife, build a rain garden, and try eco-friendly STEAM projects. Kids will get messy, ask questions, and take home a handmade piece plus new nature skills.
description_source: Family art-making event inspired by Earth Day, featuring outdoor art projects, nature walks, wildlife encounters, eco-friendly STEAM activities, rain garden building, photo treasure hunts, and guided bird walks. Activities run from 1 PM to 4 PM on April 25, 2026, at the Manes Art Education Center. Open to all ages; children under 18 must be accompanied by an adult.

title: Earth Day Beach Cleanup
tagline: Help keep our shoreline clean!
description: Help clean the shoreline at Sands Point Preserve by joining a community beach cleanup where volunteers get gloves, bags and guidance from environmental educators. Kids ages 5–12 can hunt for plastic, learn about local wildlife and try simple sorting and recycling tasks. Admission to the Preserve is free for volunteers. It’s a hands-on, family-friendly way for kids to see how small actions help the environment.
description_source: Join a community beach cleanup at Sands Point Preserve to remove plastic and harmful debris from the shoreline. Volunteers will be provided with cleanup materials and guidance from environmental educators. Free admission to the Preserve is offered to all participants. Event includes educational components about environmental conservation.

title: 5th Annual Broadway Celebrates Earth Day Concert
tagline: A celebration of climate action and Broadway talent
description: Head to Duffy Square for the 5th Annual Broadway Celebrates Earth Day Concert — a free outdoor show with Broadway artists plus more than 100 students and community performers. Family-friendly activities and exhibits from The Climate Museum let kids see climate action up close, try hands-on displays, and join sing-alongs. Good for families with children (0–12) as part of NYC’s Car-Free Earth Day.
description_source: The 5th Annual Broadway Celebrates Earth Day Concert is a free public event hosted by the Broadway Green Alliance and Times Square Alliance, featuring live performances by Broadway artists and over 100 students and community performers. The event includes family-friendly activities and exhibits from The Climate Museum, held in Duffy Square as part of NYC’s Car-Free Earth Day initiative.

title: Car-Free Earth Day 2026
tagline: Celebrate sustainability with activities and games!
description: Car-Free Earth Day closes Broadway around Flatiron Plaza for a free, family-friendly street festival. Kids ages 5–12 can try climate-focused games, join a scavenger hunt in Madison Square Park, and explore hands-on exhibits and a pop-up park from ASLA-NY. It’s a relaxed way for children to learn about design and sustainability while playing.
description_source: Car-Free Earth Day is an annual event hosted by NYC DOT, closing Broadway from 31st to 20th Streets to vehicular traffic. ASLA-NY and Flatiron NoMad Partnership host a pop-up park, outdoor exhibits of design award winners, educational activities, and climate-focused games. title: Maurice Duruflé: The Complete Organ Works Plus
tagline: A performance of Duruflé's complete organ works and chamber music.
description: At Brick Presbyterian Church, Raymond Nagem and Melissa Brassard play Maurice Duruflé’s complete organ works, joined by flutist Bart Feller and violist Kal Sugatski for his rarely heard Prélude, Récitatif, et Variations, Op. 3. Good for families with kids 10 and up. Children will hear how organ, flute and viola blend and how composers shape quiet and loud moments. Tickets $35, $15 students.
description_source: Raymond Nagem and Melissa Brassard, joined by flutist Bart Feller and violist Kal Sugatski, perform Maurice Duruflé’s complete organ works and his only chamber composition, the rarely performed Prélude, Récitatif, et Variations, Op. 3, for flute, viola, and piano. Duruflé was a perfectionist who published only a handful of meticulously crafted works during his career.

title: ICP Family Art Hour: Paper Crane Cyanotype
tagline: Hands-on family workshop exploring art and history
description: Make paper cranes and print cyanotypes in a hands-on family workshop at the International Center of Photography. Led by educator Carlos Nunez, kids (ages 4–12) will fold, expose blueprints, and explore stories about family and belonging. The session includes a short tour of ICP’s exhibitions; free with museum admission.
description_source: Join a hands-on family workshop led by educator Carlos Nunez, inspired by the exhibition 'Haruka Sakaguchi – The Camps America Built'. Families will learn to fold paper cranes and create cyanotypes to explore family histories, personal narratives, and belonging. The event includes an introductory tour of ICP’s exhibitions.

title: FAD Market at MoMA PS1 50th Anniversary Block Party
tagline: A celebration of art, creativity, and community
description: FAD Market joins MoMA PS1’s 50th Anniversary Block Party with 60+ local artists, food stalls, DJs and live performances. Families can jump into hands-on workshops—quilting, mural-making and even self-defense—so kids get to make, move and try new skills. It’s free and open to the public, with access to Greater New York 2026.
description_source: FAD Market makes its Queens debut at MoMA PS1 during the museum’s 50th Anniversary Block Party, coinciding with the opening weekend of Greater New York 2026. The event features over 60 local artists and makers, family-friendly hands-on workshops (including quilting, mural-making, and self-defense), food from Queens-based vendors, DJ sets, live performances, and access to the Greater New York 2026 exhibition. Free and open to the public.

title: Wild Wonders (Bronx Zoo)
tagline: Explore wildlife through play!
description: Wild Wonders at the Bronx Zoo is a four-part program for children 10 months to 3 years that pairs one adult with one child for short outdoor walks, up-close animal encounters, and guided exhibit time. Sessions include seasonal games, art, and sensory play so little ones can touch, hear, and see animals while building curiosity and motor skills. Series cost: $200 ($180 members).
description_source: Wild Wonders is a program for children aged 10 months to 3 years, featuring up-close animal encounters, guided exhibit tours, and interactive seasonal activities including games, art, and sensory play. The program includes walking outside and is designed as a 4-part series. Each session accommodates one adult and one child.

title: Tot Time Tuesdays
tagline: Free toddler-friendly crafts, storytime, and guided hike at Shirley Chisholm State Park.
description: Bring toddlers to Shirley Chisholm State Park on Tuesday mornings for free crafts, a 10:30 AM storytime, and a short guided hike at 11. title: Clown & Puppet Show for Kids
tagline: An interactive spectacle for kids and families!
description: Join SWOOPZ the Clown and a cast of puppet characters for a 90-minute interactive show at Young Ethel’s in Brooklyn. Kids ages 3–10 can shout suggestions, watch performances by Prism, Borbo and friends, and get balloon twists from Kooky Cosmo. Tickets are $5–$10 and non-alcoholic drinks are available, making it a hands-on, playful outing.

title: Free Second Sundays at the Whitney
tagline: Free admission and all-ages activities every second Sunday of the month
description: The Whitney opens free every second Sunday of the month, with family-friendly tours, hands-on artmaking, and themed programs for kids (ages 0–12). It’s a relaxed way for little ones to try making art, explore galleries, and join guided activities at the museum in Manhattan. Entry is free but you need a timed ticket, available one month ahead.
description_source: Admission to the Whitney Museum of American Art is free all day on the second Sunday of every month. Visitors of all ages can access all exhibitions, participate in artmaking activities, attend guided tours, and join specially themed events. Tickets are required due to limited capacity and are available one month in advance.

title: Spring Family Discovery Weekends
tagline: Explore nature together!
description: Bring kids to drop-in, hands-on stations across the meadow, woodland, marsh, and vegetable garden at Brooklyn Botanic Garden. Led by volunteer educators and teen apprentices, children ages 3–12 can touch, observe, and try simple nature activities that teach about plants and habitats. Runs weekends 10:30–12:30 Apr 11–Jun 7 (some dates excluded); outdoors and free with garden admission (under 5 free), canceled in rain or if under 50°F.
description_source: Drop-in hands-on stations in the meadow, woodland, marsh, and vegetable garden allow families to explore nature alongside volunteer educators and teenage Garden apprentices. Programs run Saturdays and Sundays from 10:30 a.m. to 12:30 p.m. between April 11 and June 7, 2026, except May 2, 16, 23–24. All activities are outdoors and canceled in inclement weather (rain or temperatures below 50°F).
subway: 2/3/4/5 to Franklin Ave; 2/3 to Eastern Parkway-Brooklyn Museum
price_summary: Free with garden admission; children under 5 enter free.

title: Watson Adventures’ Grand Central Scramble Family Scavenger Hunt
tagline: Uncover the secrets of America’s most amazing train station!
description: This family scavenger hunt sends teams through Grand Central Terminal to solve photo challenges and uncover little-known stories. Kids (ages 7–14) will explore the Whispering Gallery, study the celestial ceiling, find celebrity photos and spot hidden animals while practicing observation and teamwork. Tickets $17.50–$21.50; private bookings vary.
description_source: Families work together to solve photo challenges and uncover hidden history in Grand Central Terminal. Activities include navigating the Whispering Gallery, identifying celestial patterns in the ceiling mural, locating celebrity photos in the food court, standing on fish beneath an upside-down tree in the Grand Central Market, and counting hidden animal figures near a ship motif. Teams explore architectural details and historical artifacts across the terminal. Designed for collaborative exploration.
price_summary: Tickets priced between $17.50 and $21.50; private bookings may vary.

title: Family Hours
tagline: Free monthly workshops for kids and caregivers
description: Bring kids ages 4–12 and a caregiver to Swiss Institute’s free, drop-in Family Hours on the second Saturday of each month. Artist-led, hands-on projects inspired by current exhibitions let children experiment with materials, try simple techniques, and explore eco-conscious ideas. Come any time during the session, but RSVP by email — admission isn't guaranteed without a reservation.
description_source: Free monthly drop-in workshops on the second Saturday of each month for children ages 4–12 and their caregivers, featuring hands-on, artist-led art-making activities inspired by current exhibitions. Activities emphasize environmentally conscious themes and change monthly. Families may arrive at any time during scheduled hours. RSVP required via email; admission not guaranteed without reservation.

title: Family Art Project: Turtle Friends
tagline: Craft your own shell backpack!
description: At Wave Hill families can learn how turtles wake from winter and see the different shapes, sizes, and patterns of their shells. Kids ages 3–12 will make a fabric shell backpack with materials provided, trying simple cutting, gluing, and stitching. Children get a wearable craft to take home. No registration; free with grounds admission.
description_source: As turtles wake up from their winter rest, learn about their fascinating lives, including the diverse shapes, sizes, and patterns of their shells. Craft a shell backpack using provided fabric materials. Registration not required.

title: KIDS: Light, Form and Shadow Workshop with Jim Gingerich
tagline: Learn a Lifelong Skill Set: Light, Form & Shadow in Six Simple Steps
description: Kids 5–12 learn to draw and paint by watching how light hits a simple sphere. They’ll practice drawing a circle, placing the cast shadow, and using light and shadow to make the shape look three-dimensional. Materials are provided and the workshop at Southampton Arts Center costs $0–$5.
description_source: Students aged 5–12 will learn to draw and paint by observing how light interacts with form and shadow, using a simple sphere as a subject. The workshop teaches foundational skills: drawing a circle to represent the sphere, mapping its cast shadow as an oval, and translating observed light patterns into a three-dimensional image. Materials provided.

tagline: Join the Urban Park Rangers for a nature scavenger hunt inspired by the popular mobile game application 'Pokemon Go'.

Children search for the real rodents, insects, birds and reptiles that helped shape Pokémon, using hands-on ID materials to learn about local wildlife. A family-friendly Kids Week activity during Spring Break.
description_source: Join the Urban Park Rangers for a nature scavenger hunt inspired by the popular mobile game application 'Pokemon Go'. Participants explore Wolfe’s Pond Park to identify real-world rodents, insects, birds, and reptiles that inspired the design of Pokémon creatures. Educational materials provided. Event is part of NYC Parks' Kids Week during Spring Break.
subway: S44, S54, S74 to Staten Island; nearest stop: Arthur Kill Road or Richmond Terrace
address: Entrance - Cornelia Avenue and Chester Avenue, Staten Island, NY
reviews: ["My 7-year-old loved spotting real birds and bugs while hunting for 'Pokémon' clues. The rangers were patient and knowledgeable.", 'Great way to get kids outside during spring break. The educational twist made it more engaging than a typical scavenger hunt.', 'Well-organized and informative. My 10-year-old learned about local wildlife without realizing it was a lesson. Highly recommend.']

title: Upcycled Egg Carton (and Cardboard) Crafts
tagline: Create springtime critters from recycled materials!
description: Bring kids to the Greenbelt Nature Center for a hands-on workshop turning egg cartons and cardboard into springtime insects with paint and markers. Suitable for ages 3–12 (younger kids should have a caregiver). Materials and guidance are provided, and there’ll be a short outdoor break if weather allows—wear clothes that can get messy. Free.

title: Silly Billy Comedy Magic Show
tagline: A highly interactive comedy magic show
description: Silly Billy is a fast-paced, interactive comedy magic show where kids join in on illusions and silly bits. Suited for ages 3–10 (best for 5–10), the award-winning performer gets children laughing and trying simple magic tricks themselves. The show runs at Staten Island Children's Museum and is included with museum admission — free to attend.
description_source: Silly Billy performs an interactive comedy magic show featuring magical illusions and humorous acts designed for children. He is a multiple award-winning magician, named New York Magician of the Year and Best Family Performer. The show is included with museum admission.

title: TEDxCUNY 2026: PLAYBACK
tagline: Rewind, reflect, and reimagine
description: TEDxCUNY 2026: PLAYBACK is a day of short talks at the Gerald W. Lynch Theater in NYC that look back at personal and public stories to help rethink the future. Talks are idea-driven and approachable, so older kids and teens can follow and take part in conversations. Families will hear stories, new perspectives, and practical ideas to discuss at home. Free for CUNY and non‑CUNY faculty and staff.

title: Jake Xerxes Fussell, Sam Amidon, Jana Horn
tagline: A night of experimental folk music
description: Three contemporary folk artists—Jake Xerxes Fussell, Sam Amidon, and Jana Horn—perform three back-to-back sets at Pioneer Works in Brooklyn. They blend traditional songs with experimental arrangements, so kids and adults can hear familiar melodies reshaped in surprising ways. All ages welcome; tickets start at $25.
description_source: A live performance featuring folk artists Jake Xerxes Fussell (North Carolina singer-songwriter), Sam Amidon (Vermont-based multi-instrumentalist), and Jana Horn (Austin singer-songwriter), exploring the experimental fringes of American folk music. The event includes three distinct sets in sequence.

title: Seining Kickoff Party
tagline: Kick off our seasonal seining program!
description: At Randall’s Island Park, the seasonal seining kickoff lets participants 12 and up strap on waders and help pull a 30-foot seine along the Harlem River to find fish, crabs and jellyfish. Teens will see and ID real marine life and try water-themed crafts; all gear is provided. Wear clothes that can get wet—observers of any age are welcome. Free.
description_source: Join us at Randall’s Island Park to kick off the seasonal seining program. All attendees may participate in water-themed crafts and activities. All equipment provided; attendees must wear clothes that can get wet. Observers of all ages are welcome.

title: Early Birds of the Greenbelt Hike
tagline: Hike in search of early migrants
description: Take a morning hike on Staten Island's Greenbelt to spot early migratory birds like Phoebes, Flickers, and Hermit Thrushes (Bluebirds show up sometimes). Kids aged 5–12 will learn to listen and look for bird calls, practice steady walking on easy trails, and get hands-on nature time. Free meeting at the Greenbelt Nature Center.

title: Chapter 0: Immersive Art Scavenger Hunt
tagline: An immersive scavenger hunt in the library!
description: Join an after-hours immersive art scavenger hunt at Merrick Library for families. A mysterious book has scattered chapter pages through large-scale installations and sensory environments, and kids (ages 5–10) search the rooms to restore the story. Expect interactive audio-visual effects, hands-on exploration, and playful problem-solving that builds observation and teamwork. Free; registration required.

Includes interactive audio-visual effects and exploration of themed installations.

Sessions are canceled on February 20, March 27, April 3, April 10, and May 1. Pre-registration is required due to limited space.

title: Community Event: Tech Together at William Floyd STEAM Night
tagline: Hands-on technology exhibit for K-2 students and their families
description: Bring K–2 kids to Tech Together at William Floyd High School for a hands-on technology exhibit. What does it mean to be someone's child?

title: Ready, Set, Kindergarten!
tagline: A special six-session series for early learning.
description: Bring your 3–5 year-old to a six-session series at the Sheepshead Bay Library where stories, music, art and play introduce early literacy, science and math. Kids will listen, sing, count, explore and make simple projects that build school-ready skills. Families receive free take-home materials to keep the fun and learning going at home. It's free.

title: Raising Emotionally Healthy Kids with Dr. Becky Kennedy
tagline: A skills-based approach to parenting
description: Join Dr. Becky Kennedy at Lenox Hill Hospital for a free, practical session on handling kids' big emotions, setting boundaries without punishment, and supporting emotional health. This is aimed at parents of young children and offers clear, skills-based tools you can try right away. Your child may end up feeling more seen, secure, and better able to calm down.
description_source: Welcome to Lenox Hill Hospital's Center for Family Education Community Health Series! title: Saturday Science: From A to ZI
tagline: Explore the workings of the brain through hands-on activities and unique scientific resources.
description: Bring kids ages 5–12 to a free monthly Saturday Science session at Columbia’s Jerome L. Greene Science Center. They’ll try hands-on neuroscience activities from BioBus and CUNO, doing simple experiments and demos to see how the brain works. This month’s theme nods to the Zuckerman Institute (“ZI”); registration is required.
description_source: A free monthly event for kids of all ages to explore the workings of the brain through hands-on activities and unique scientific resources. Every month we offer new activities, created by our partners BioBus and CUNO (Columbia University Neuroscience Outreach). Our activities are designed for children ages 5-12, but learners of all ages are invited to join scientists in the Zuckerman Institute’s Education Lab, located at 605 West 129th Street.

title: 2025 - Volunteer at a STEM Weekend Enrichment Program for NYC Youth
tagline: Support youth with a free day of STEM activities!
description: Help run free Saturday STEM sessions at The Parris Foundation in NYC, supporting students in grades 6–12 with hands-on workshops, mentorship, and team-building. title: 2026 Kieve Wavus Loyalty Fund Party
tagline: Join community members to support kids going to camp!
description: Join community members at the annual Kieve Wavus Loyalty Fund Party to raise money for camp financial aid during KWE’s 100th year. Tickets are $70 and include an open bar and raffle tickets, with extras available to buy. This 21+ evening in New York is a chance to reconnect with friends while supporting kids who want to go to camp.
description_source: Join community members from across the country to reconnect at our Annual Kieve Wavus Loyalty Fund Party during KWE's 100th year! title: JAZZ AT ONE: Orrin Evans
tagline: Free midday jazz performances
description: Catch a free midday jazz set by pianist Orrin Evans at historic St. Paul’s Chapel as part of Trinity’s Concerts at One. Kids ages 5–12 can hear swing, blues and adventurous improvisation up close and feel the rhythms—great for little listeners curious about music and movement. Family-friendly, drop-in welcome.
description_source: As part of Trinity’s commitment to jazz, a uniquely American art form and uniting voice for our collective story and experience, Trinity Church presents Jazz at One in collaboration with JAZZ HOUSE KiDS. We are excited to announce the next installment of Jazz at One with TRINITY CHURCH as a part of Trinity’s Concerts at One series held at the historic St. Paul’s Chapel, located on Broadway and Fulton in lower Manhattan. Concerts feature a lineup of talented artists each week, exploring some of the richest traditions in American music.

title: Rockefeller Center & Space Club Present: Tiny iNDIE

description: Part of the NY Auto Show at the Javits Center, the Mobility Grid Summit looks at autonomous driving, vehicle-to-grid tech and EV batteries as home energy. title: Painting With Bioluminescent Bacteria
tagline: Join us to paint with microbes!
description: Paint with glowing bacteria (Vibrio fischeri) at Genspace in Brooklyn in this hands-on family workshop. Kids will pipette and “paint” on agar plates, watch growth form into glowing patterns, and learn how bioluminescence works. Suited for ages 7–12 (5+ OK with adult); entries can be submitted to the Agar Art contest. Free but register.
description_source: In this workshop, we’ll explore bioluminescence in nature, and use it as a material to create living art and design. You’ll design your own Vibrio fischeri bioluminescent bacteria culture and use experimental art techniques to design the growth and shape of your culture. This event is part of the American Society for Microbiology Agar Art contest, and all participants are invited to enter their microbial art for the chance to win a prize!
price_summary: Free event, but registration is required

title: Two Young Girls
tagline: A one-act meditation on public space and private terror
description: This one-act play takes place on a subway and follows two girls as other riders react—or don’t—to a tense moment. It's a quiet, thought-provoking look at how people notice (or ignore) trouble in public. Good for ages 10–16; kids will see different responses and get ideas for talking about bystander choices. Free at Under St. Mark’s Theater in NYC.
description_source: A one-act play set on a subway train, exploring why people look away when something is wrong in plain sight.

title: Chase Montana: From Funny to Fatherhood (A New Yorker's Story)
tagline: One man. One Microphone. And one little reason to grow the hell up.
description: Chase Montana’s one-man show at The Rat NYC in Brooklyn blends stand-up, personal storytelling and short film as he traces life from an orphaned street hustler to the moment he became a father. Expect frank language and sensitive, sometimes raw scenes that move between dark humor and emotional reflection. You’ll hear intimate stories and see filmed moments that dig into fatherhood. Recommended for adults 18+; tickets $17.85.

title: NYPL Green: Pollinator Puppet-Making with Esther Crow
tagline: Make puppets and learn about pollinators and their role in ecosystems.
description: Bring kids to the Battery Park City Library for a hands-on puppet workshop with Esther Crow and Bernie the Bee. Children ages 5–12 will make bee, bat, and butterfly puppets from recycled materials and learn how pollinators help our food and ecosystems. Materials provided; bringing cardboard tubes or fabric scraps is welcome.
description_source: In this workshop, Esther and her puppet, Bernie the Bee, will assist kids in creating bee, bat and butterfly puppets and will teach about how these creatures pollinate many of the fruits and vegetables we eat, and how important they are to maintaining healthy ecosystems. Materials will be provided, but kids are encouraged to bring recycled toilet paper and paper towel rolls for the base of the puppets, and any other recycled/upcycled materials such as colored paper, fabric scraps, beads, sequins...

title: SPRING SLAM: Fresh Start Snack Drive

We are collecting imperishable healthy foods & snacks like fruit snacks, nuts, granola bars, and more.

title: Increasing Access to Evidence-Based Treatment for Common Mental Health Problems in Childhood
tagline: Improving interventions for childhood mental health
description: A talk on improving access to evidence-based treatments for child mental health, using child anxiety as the example. It looks at parent-led approaches and digital tools that help get care to families quickly. Best for parents, clinicians, and families with children aged 6–16; $25 at the Child Mind Institute in NYC.
description_source: Across the world the demand for effective interventions for mental health problems in children and young people far exceeds supply. In this talk I will use the example of child anxiety problems to illustrate how we have made the most of parents and of digital approaches to increase access to effective psychological treatments, with a particular focus on how we can make effective treatments available to families when they first need them.

title: Gaia Open Mic: Showers/Flowers
tagline: A unique opportunity to play with an audience.
description: An open mic night themed Showers/Flowers where kids (5+) and adults share songs, poems and playful performances. Children can try singing, drumming, or building a collaborative soundscape using the provided keyboard, guitars and mic, and learn how to lead a call-and-response with the audience. Free entry — no one turned away for lack of funds at Arts On Site NYC.
description_source: Join us for an open mic night where all forms of art are welcome! Experience the joy of creating with an audience as we share songs, poems, and creative expressions. The theme this month is Showers/Flowers, where we invite you to bring art of your highs and lows, botanical or otherwise. This open mic is a unique opportunity to play with an audience, not just for them. Come experience the joys of getting the audience singing your original music, creating a collaborative soundscape for your poem, or inviting them to drum along to your story! title: Foodies + New Friends: Brooklyn | College Kids Edition
tagline: Make connections over dinner with new friends!
description: Small-group dinners for 18–24 year olds in Brooklyn that seat 4–6 college students together at a rotating local restaurant. Food and drinks are not included; attendees pay for their own meals. Organized to reduce loneliness and support local businesses.

title: «How Many Animals Can Fit in This Book?» Natalia Yaskina
tagline: Read-Aloud + Create Your Own Animal Collage Workshop
description: Hear a lively read-aloud of Natalia Yaskina’s picture book at Kvartira Books in Brooklyn, then make and decorate your own animal collage from pre-painted cardboard parts. Suited for ages 3–8 (older kids welcome), children get hands-on practice with cutting, arranging and creative play while exploring sharing and personal space. Book signing follows; $33.85.
description_source: Just how many animals can fit in this book? Ant will tell you loud and clear: the answer is ONE. But will Ant mind if other animals join the pages, too? Two lions? Four zebras? How about eight penguins? A humorous picture book that cleverly shows that there is enough room for everyone – while acknowledging that, sometimes, we may need our own space. Ideal for ages 3–8, though older children (8–12) are welcome.

title: Family Storytime in Japanese
tagline: Join children's librarian, Nobuko Wilson, for a fun storytime in Japanese!

Kids (infants–age 5) will hear short stories, sing and move along, and practice listening in Japanese. Free but space is limited to 25 children; register at the desk or by phone starting the Tuesday of that week; happens monthly on the third Sunday.

title: Family Art Making: Portraits of Identity
tagline: Create your own portrait inspired by art
description: Explore portraits on view at MoMA and then make your own using collage and drawing in this drop-in family art session at the Cullman Education Building. Kids (5+) can play with symbols, costumes and color to tell a personal story through images. Children will learn simple portrait techniques and how artists use imagery to express identity. Free with advance registration; museum admission and supplies included.
description_source: How do artists tell their stories? Take inspiration from the symbols, costumes, and settings in portraits on view in Frida and Diego: The Last Dream, Ideas of Africa: Portraiture and Political Imagination, and Wifredo Lam: When I Don’t Sleep I Dream. Then use collage and drawing techniques to make a portrait of your own in this drop-in art-making session. Recommended for families with kids ages five and up. This program is free with advance registration and includes admission to the Museum.

title: Vander Ende-Onderdonk House Museum Tours
tagline: Explore New York City's oldest Dutch fieldstone house
description: Bring the family to the Vander Ende-Onderdonk House, a 1709 Dutch fieldstone house in Ridgewood, for weekend guided tours through period rooms and Ridgewood’s story. Kids 5–12 can try a hands-on scavenger hunt, spotting artifacts and solving clues to learn about daily life long ago. Admission is $5; donations welcome.
description_source: The Vander Ende-Onderdonk House, built in 1709, is New York City’s oldest Dutch fieldstone house and operates as a community museum. Open Saturdays and Sundays from 12:00 PM to 5:00 PM, it offers guided tours of three floors of period rooms and exhibits detailing 18th- and 19th-century life, including the history of the Vander Ende and Onderdonk families and Ridgewood’s transformation from farmland to industrial neighborhood. Admission is $5 per person. School group visits (Tuesdays/Thursdays) include guided tours and 18th-century craft activities.

description: Hands-on weekday program at King Manor Museum about 18th-century food practices. description_source: Join King Manor for special weekday programming during the New York City school break! What was a kitchen like before refrigeration and supermarkets? title: JAZZ AT ONE: Silvano Monasterios Venezuelan Nonet
tagline: Soul-moving midday music
description: Pianist Silvano Monasterios brings his 2025 work The River to a nine-piece ensemble for a free midday Jazz at One concert at St. Paul’s Chapel. Expect live jazz with strong Latin and Afro-Caribbean rhythms, lively percussion and big-band colors. Families with kids 0–12 can listen closely, clap or sway along, and hear how jazz blends melody and rhythm.
description_source: As part of Trinity’s commitment to jazz, a uniquely American art form and uniting voice for our collective story and experience, Trinity Church presents Jazz at One in collaboration with JAZZ HOUSE KiDS. We are excited to announce the next installment of Jazz at One with TRINITY CHURCH as a part of Trinity’s Concerts at One series held at the historic St. Paul’s Chapel, located on Broadway and Fulton in lower Manhattan. Concerts feature a lineup of talented artists each week, exploring some of the rich cultural traditions in jazz.

title: Earth Day 2026 Festival
tagline: Celebrate Earth with activities for kids and families!
description: Head to Union Square for the Earth Day 2026 Festival, a free, family-friendly outdoor event with live performances, climate art, and sustainable food vendors. Dozens of environmental nonprofits and campaigns will have booths where you can ask questions and find ways to get involved. Kids can try hands-on workshops and crafts that teach simple green habits and sample planet-friendly snacks.
description_source: Earth Day is here! Check out dozens of green groups, live entertainment, kids' activities, climate art, workshops, and sustainable foods. Join us this April at the Earth Day 2026 Festival in New York City’s Union Square. Our annual in-person festival features dozens of environmental non-profits and climate campaigns, interactive workshops, and kids’ activities. Learn how to live greener, get involved in environmental causes, try new planet-friendly foods and drinks, and find ways to connect with your community year-round.

title: 1 PM: Mozart for Munchkins: Peter and the Wolf (and Friends)
tagline: A playful, interactive retelling of Prokofiev’s musical tale
description: This family concert at St. Ann & the Holy Trinity Church offers a playful, interactive retelling of Prokofiev’s Peter and the Wolf by bilingual duo Musiquita! (English/Spanish). Kids ages 3–8 can follow the story through music and movement—trumpet, bass, hula-hoop contortion and tap dance—and then try instruments in a petting zoo. title: Classical4Kids
tagline: A music listening event for families
description: Pianist Eleonor Bindman leads a casual family listening session at the Old Stone House of Brooklyn where kids (ages 5–12) hear curated classical playlists available on major streaming services. Children follow simple listening prompts and do coloring activities that sharpen musical attention and rhythm awareness. It’s free and gives parents easy ideas for music at home.
description_source: Classical pianist Eleonor Bindman hosts this music listening event for families. Parents are invited to support their children's music education at home through a regular music-listening experience. Eleonor will share curated playlists available across all major streaming platforms, making it easy to bring high-quality music into everyday routines. In addition to listening, families can enjoy simple coloring activities and guided listening prompts that encourage musical awareness.

title: Bike Skills 101: Learn to Ride-Kids 1PM

title: Bike Skills 101: Learn to Ride-Kids 11AM
tagline: Learn to ride for the first time!
description: Bring kids ages 5–12 to a free, hands-on class in Sunset Park where instructors help them learn to ride without training wheels. Children practice balancing, pedaling, starting, stopping and steering through simple drills and one-on-one tips. Bring your child’s bike and helmet; the class runs along 5th Ave between 41st and 45th.
description_source: Bring your kids for a fun morning of learning bike basics and gaining confidence on two wheels! This free group class is for children who are ready to ditch the training wheels and ride a two-wheeler for the first time. With our safe, easy, effective method taught by experienced instructors, kids will learn how to balance, pedal, start, stop, and steer a bicycle. Registration for this class should be under the student's name, not that of parent or guardian. You must bring your child's bike and helmet to this class. This class will take place on 5th Ave between 41st and 45th Street in Sunset Park, Brooklyn. This Car Free Earth Day event is co-sponsored by our friends at NYC DOT. Want more information? Email us at classes@bike.nyc. See you there!

title: The Hidden Metabolic Changes Affecting Children — What Parents and Physicians Need to Know
tagline: Discover the metabolic health shifts in children and how to manage them, with focus on environmental toxins and early warning signs.
description: Join a free online webinar for parents and clinicians about changing metabolic health in kids — rising obesity, insulin resistance and early cardiovascular risks. It reviews evidence linking environmental toxins (like persistent organic pollutants) and clears up common myths about fat, cholesterol and sugar. You’ll leave with practical steps to reduce your child’s toxin exposure and support healthier metabolism.
description_source: This webinar aims to: 1. Dispel the common misconceptions that saturated fat, cholesterol and sugar are the cause of metabolic disease; 2. Get ready for a hands-on animal adventure that will deepen your connection to the creatures we share our world with.

This interactive session introduces basic hula steps, hand motions, and the meaning behind the dance while nurturing confidence, creativity, and respect for Hawaiian culture.

title: Arbor Day at the Audubon Center in Prospect Park
tagline: Join the Prospect Park Alliance to celebrate trees!
description: Spend Arbor Day at the Audubon Center in Prospect Park with hands-on nature activities for kids ages 5–10. Children plant seeds in recycled paper planters, make leaf art, and join a guided tree ID walk to see how trees support the park’s ecosystem. Free event — a relaxed, outdoor morning for families.
description_source: Join Prospect Park Alliance for a day of nature games and an exhibit on Brooklyn’s last remaining forest, Prospect Park! Featured Activities include: Sow a Seed (10–12 pm): Learn about seeds’ survival needs and plant a seed in your own custom planter made from recycled paper. Leaf Art (10–12 pm): Discover the distinctive characteristics of trees and the many shapes of their leaves while creating art inspired by nature. Nature on the Go, ID a Tree (12–1 pm): Take an in-depth look at the largest organisms within Prospect Park—the trees.

title: Car-Free Earth Day
tagline: Celebrate a car-free day with fun activities and education!
description: Streets and plazas across NYC go car-free for the day, with a main hub at Flatiron Plaza — free for everyone. Kids (ages 5–12) can play ping-pong, corn hole, Connect Four and explore a pop-up park with public art and design exhibits. Families will pick up hands-on ideas about sustainability, climate-friendly travel, and community programs.
description_source: An annual car-free event hosted by NYC DOT. Programming is presented at signature locations citywide to promote activism and education surrounding climate change, sustainability, and other relevant topics. In 2026, the event will close 54 streets and plazas, with ASLA-NY hosting a pop-up park at Flatiron Plaza featuring exhibits of Design Award Winners and educational activities.

title: Earth Week at Children's Museum of Manhattan
tagline: Exploring how things grow, change, and connect beneath and above the ground!
description: At the Children's Museum of Manhattan's Earth Week (April 14–22), kids can try hands-on nature play and art that explore how plants and animals grow and connect. For ages 3–7, highlights include a Garden of Produce sensory bin, Seed to Sky collage murals, storytime of I Hate Honey with animal crafts, and puppet shows by Esther Crow. All activities are included with free admission.

title: New Victory Family Benefit: Circus in the City

description: At New Victory Theater, Circus in the City opens with YOAH — a Japanese circus mixing aerial silks, juggling, hypnotic projections and taiko drumming fused with electronica. After the show families (ages 7–18) head to an exclusive Dave & Buster’s party with lunch, hands-on activities, arcade games and swag bags. Tickets start at $150 for kids; proceeds support the theater’s arts education programs.

Then after the show, stilt walkers lead the way to an exclusive celebration at Dave & Buster’s with lunch, hands-on activities, arcade games, swag bags and more! title: Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: The Exhibit
tagline: Step into the world of the Mickey Mouse Clubhouse!
description: Help your child join Mickey and friends at Discovery Cube LA to plan a surprise party for Pluto. Kids ages 3–8 can bake and decorate cupcakes, build bubble machines with Donald and Professor Von Drake, paint with Daisy, and grill with Goofy—hands-on play that practices creativity, teamwork and basic STEM. Free with museum admission.
description_source: At Mickey Mouse Clubhouse: The Exhibit, kids and families are invited to help Mickey, Minnie and their pals prepare a surprise birthday party for their favorite pup: Pluto! Activities include baking and decorating cupcakes with Minnie, inventing bubble-making machines with Donald and Professor Von Drake, crafting and painting with Daisy, and grilling with Goofy. It's a fun-filled experience where children can engage in various interactive activities designed to spark creativity and joy.

title: Hooray LA
tagline: Hollywood isn’t a place...It’s a puppet show!
description: A puppet show at the Bob Baker Marionette Theater that brings Los Angeles landmarks and moments to life with hundreds of handcrafted marionettes. Kids (recommended 3–10; under 2 free) will watch a fish swim the LA River, see freeway traffic and a lonely mountain lion on Griffith Park, and pick up playful bits of local history. Free admission with ticket reservation.
description_source: The people and places of the City of Angels come to puppet life in Hooray LA! A plethora of handcrafted marionettes invite you to celebrate the city, from a fish swimming through the LA River to the traffic jams on LA freeways to the mountains of Griffith Park, where one lone mountain lion looks for love… And so much more! Hooray LA! was Bob Baker’s ode to his city and the last new show he ever created. First performed in 1981 for the Los Angeles Bicentennial, the show was recently revived to in...
price_summary: Free admission; ticket reservation required. Children 2 and under free.

title: Jaws: The Exhibition
tagline: Experience the largest exhibition ever mounted for the Oscar®-winning film Jaws, celebrating its 50th anniversary.
description: See Jaws up close at the Academy Museum: the 50th‑anniversary exhibition displays original props, behind‑the‑scenes artifacts and immersive scene recreations. Kids (recommended 5–12) can step into recreated sets, try interactive displays and learn how the shark effects and filmmaking came together. Free with museum admission; visitors 17 and under enter free.
description_source: Jaws: The Exhibition is the first-ever exhibition of this scale at the Academy Museum, focused exclusively on a single film, and the largest mounted exhibition ever for Jaws, the Oscar®-winning film from Universal Pictures. It celebrates the film’s 50th anniversary and revisits Jaws scene by scene, through original objects, behind-the-scenes revelations, and interactive moments. Curated with direct access to the collections from Steven Spielberg and The Amblin Hearth Archive, NBCUniversal Archives.
price_summary: Free with museum admission; visitors 17 and under get in free.

title: Smorgasburg Los Angeles
tagline: A weekly Sunday market featuring unique food vendors & activities
description: Smorgasburg LA is a weekly Sunday outdoor market at ROW DTLA with dozens of local food vendors, design and vintage stalls, live music, and themed pop-ups and cultural events. Dozens of exciting food vendors show up each Sunday at Smorgasburg, along with vendors from the realms of design, craft, style, vintage, wellness, and more. Cultural events, pop-ups, and other surprise experiences are also part of the market mix.

title: Monterey Park Cherry Blossom Festival
tagline: Celebrate Japanese culture with fun activities
description: The 26th Monterey Park Cherry Blossom Festival at Barnes Park brings Japanese and Japanese American culture outdoors with taiko drumming, classical dance, and martial arts demonstrations. Kids can try origami and ikebana, make crafts, watch mochi being pounded, and join a tea ceremony. Free and family-friendly for all ages.
description_source: Monterey Park's Cherry Blossom Festival celebrates its 26th year in 2026. The festival is held in Barnes Park, offering attendees the chance to see, hear, and taste a bit of Japanese and Japanese American culture. Performances include classical Japanese dancing, taiko drumming, and martial arts. Guests can participate in the ancient art of the tea ceremony, observe mochi making, learn about origami and ikebana, and shop for hand-made crafts. Other highlights include games and crafts for children...

title: Disney California Adventure Food & Wine Festival
tagline: A culinary celebration with food, drinks, and fun for everyone!
description: At Disney California Adventure's Food & Wine Festival you'll find small-plate tastings, beverage seminars, chef demos and weekend family crafts with live music. Kids (ages 2–12) can sample new treats like chicharrón nachos, ramen mac & cheese or ube latte and join hands-on craft activities. Park admission required; Sip & Savor passes and paid classes are optional.
description_source: Disneyland Resort Events Page
subway: Take the train to Anaheim Station and catch a shuttle to the park
price_summary: Free entry with park admission; optional paid experiences include Sip and Savor Passes ($34–$64), VIP tastings ($150), and cooking classes ($75).

title: Studio Ghibli's PONYO
tagline: Celebrate the art of hand-drawn animation
description: At the Academy Museum, this Ponyo exhibition shows over 100 original artworks, animation desks, and an immersive set inspired by the film. Kids can get close to hand-drawn cels and try an animation table to build short sequences. It’s family-friendly and free for youth 17 and under with museum admission. Expect hands-on moments and a clear look at how animation is made.
description_source: Current Exhibitions at the Academy Museum
price_summary: Free with museum admission; admission free for youth 17 and under

title: Mummies of the World: The Exhibition
tagline: Explore real human and animal mummies from ancient Egypt, South America, and Europe through scientific analysis and CT scans.
description: See more than 30 real human and animal mummies from ancient Egypt, South America and Europe at the California Science Center. CT scans and hands-on displays show how researchers uncover health, diet and burial customs — kids (recommended 6–12) can play archaeology detective. Admission varies by age; under 3 free and the visit includes the IMAX film Mummies 3D.
description_source: Mummies of the World: The Exhibition detailed information from California Science Center
address: 700 Exposition Park Dr, Los Angeles, CA 90037
price_summary: Adults: $28; Students & Seniors: $24; Children: $20; Children under 3 free.

title: Family Flicks: Singin' in the Rain
tagline: Free family movie screening featuring classic Hollywood musicals
description: Bring the family to a free outdoor screening of Singin' in the Rain at the Hammer Museum, co-presented by the UCLA Film & Television Archive. Kids 6+ can watch Gene Kelly and Debbie Reynolds' lively song-and-dance scenes, sing along, laugh, and see how films moved from silent pictures to talkies. A relaxed, kid-friendly intro to classic Hollywood.
description_source: The museum will close at 6 p.m. on Friday, April 3 and be closed all day Saturday, April 4 in advance of the Spring Exhibitions Opening. Join us for the opening. ScreeningsKids. Co-presented by the UCLA Film & Television Archive. Recommended for ages 6+. Gene Kelly and Debbie Reynolds shine in perhaps the greatest Hollywood musical of all time. Propelled by a crackling script and exuberantly delightful song-and-dance routines, Kelly is a silent movie star making a trying transition to the talkie...

title: Bob Baker Day 2026
tagline: Join us for the 12th Annual Bob Baker Day at LA State Historic Park!
description: Celebrate puppeteer Bob Baker at Los Angeles State Historic Park with a 10am parade and an outdoor carnival. Kids (ages 1–12) can watch puppet shows, join crafts and music, and try hands-on activities that explore "recovery through the arts." Free with RSVP; a $25 donation is suggested.
description_source: The Annual Bob Baker Day celebrates the life and legacy of LA's iconic puppeteer. Held at Los Angeles State Historic Park, this year's theme is recovery through the arts. The event kicks off with a parade at 10am and features an outdoor carnival with music, entertainment, fun activities, special guests, and a plethora of puppets.

title: Tiny Tales and Mini Makers
tagline: Engaging storytime and creative activities for toddlers and preschoolers
description: Tiny Tales and Mini Makers at the Autry is a 60-minute Sunday program for ages 2–5. Kids hear a read-aloud picture book, join a lively sing-along, then make a simple craft connected to the story. It’s hands-on and stroller-friendly, so little ones practice listening, movement, and creativity. Free with museum admission.
description_source: On select Sundays, the Autry welcomes families for fun-filled mornings designed for the youngest museum lovers! Tiny Tales and Mini Makers invites toddlers, preschoolers, and their families to cozy up together for an engaging storytime followed by simple, hands-on creating. tagline: Celebrate the stories of immigrants from around the world through books, snacks, and historical activities.
description: On the final day of The New‑York Sari at the New‑York Historical Society, families can read chapter and picture books about immigrant experiences, enjoy snacks, and try hands-on historical activities. description_source: Join us for the last day of The New York Sari exhibition when we celebrate the stories of immigrants from around the world. title: The New York Sari Sunday Family Fun Day
tagline: Celebrate the New York Sari exhibition with crafts and storytelling!
description: Celebrate The New York Sari at the New-York Historical Society with a family day of storytelling and hands-on textile crafts. Author Jyoti Tajan Gopal will read My Paati’s Saris, and kids (ages 3–12) can try print-making and learn how saris are worn across regions. Free with museum admission; pick up the Family Guide for an interactive tour.
description_source: Join us for a day of family programs celebrating The New York Sari exhibition! Meet special guest, author Jyoti Tajan Gopal, as we read her book 'My Paati's Saris.' Try your hand at print making with a special textile craft, and learn about the different ways saris are worn in different regions of the world. Discover every aspect of this exhibition with The New York Sari Family Guide, as well as special editions of Reading into History and Hablemos.

title: Hablemos: The New York Sari
tagline: Explore the cultural impact of the sari
description: Visit The New York Historical for Hablemos: The New York Sari, a guided tour that looks at how saris are made and what they mean in South Asian cultures. Kids ages 5–12 (must be with an adult) then stamp their own textile using patterns and colors from the show and enjoy a snack. Free with registration — a hands-on way for children to explore fabric, color, and stories.
description_source: New York City is a kaleidoscope of colors and patterns just like a sari. This traditional garment from South Asia is the protagonist of the exhibition 'The New York Sari'. In this edition of Hablemos, we’ll be looking at the cultural impact of this garment. Throughout the guided tour, we’ll learn about their creation, its use, and its meaning. Once you’ve been inspired by the saris in the exhibition, stamp your own sari taking inspiration from the patterns and colors in the exhibition.

title: Sunday Story Time: A Visit from My Paati’s Saris author Jyoti Rajan Gopal
tagline: Kick off New York Sari Family Day with a read aloud and sari wrapping demonstration
description: Author Jyoti Rajan Gopal reads My Paati’s Saris at The New York Historical, then demonstrates how to wrap a sari. Kids ages 2–10 will hear about the colors, folds and cultural meaning and get to design their own sari inspired by the illustrations. Admission $6–$24; children under 5 free.
description_source: Join author Jyoti Rajan Gopal for a read aloud and sari wrapping demonstration as part of New York Sari Family Day! In _My Paati’s Saris_, with illustrations by Art Twink, a young Tamil boy helps his grandmother to prepare for a celebration. Throughout the day, he takes comfort in the beautiful colors, folds, and patterns of Paati’s saris and delights in expressing his true self. After the story, design your own sari, inspired by the colors and patterns in the book!

title: Spring Fling 2026
tagline: Benefitting the Parrish Art Museum’s Education Programs
description: An evening of art, dance, music and food at the Parrish Art Museum benefiting its education programs. The night includes the 73rd annual Student Exhibition and marks the 10th anniversary of ACCESS PARRISH, which supports community members with neurodivergent, physical, and cognitive disabilities. Suitable for adults and supporters of local arts. Tickets start at $200 for members.
description_source: Join us for Spring Fling! An evening of art, dance, music, and food in support of the Parrish Art Museum’s educational programming. This year we celebrate the 10th anniversary of ACCESS PARRISH, created to support our year-round community members living with a range of neurodivergent, physical, and cognitive disabilities, promoting health and wellbeing, social connection, and curiosity. The event features the Museum’s 73rd annual Student Exhibition, showcasing the next generation of East End art...

title: Branches in Bloom: A Spring Festival Celebrating Arbor Day

Admission $30 per car or $5 per person by train; under 3 free.

title: Animal Care Trainee
tagline: Help care for APEC’s animals
description: Kids ages 8–10 spend a morning at Alley Pond Environmental Center learning to care for animal ambassadors through hands-on tasks like feeding, brushing, and cleaning. title: Orienteering Meet at West Hills County Park – North
tagline: Test your map reading skills in a fun scavenger hunt!
description: At West Hills County Park – North, this orienteering meet lets families learn map reading and hunt for hidden control flags. Kids (age 5+) can try an easy 3 km trail course while older kids and adults can tackle 5 km+ woodland routes; beginners get instruction and maps. Entry is $20 ($10 members).
description_source: Join us for an exciting Orienteering Meet where you can sharpen your map reading skills while enjoying the great outdoors! First, you'll learn to orient the map and then find hidden control flags during a walk in the lovely West Hills County Park. Our courses range from easy (all controls on trails about 3K) to difficult (controls in the woods 5K+), with instructions offered for beginners. Registration is from 10:30 am-1 pm, and all competitors must finish by 3:30 pm.

title: Storie Storie: An African Storytelling Experience Live on Broadway
tagline: An African Storytelling Experience
description: Hear lively African folktales with storyteller Alim Kamara at The Theatre Center in Times Square. The performance blends folklore, history, humor, and life lessons in a way kids aged 5 and up can follow and enjoy. Children will laugh, join in call-and-response moments, and come away with new stories and cultural insights. Tickets: $20 kids, $40 adults.
description_source: On Saturday, April 25th, 2026, the tradition of African storytelling takes center stage at The Theatre Center in Times Square, bringing the heart of the continent’s oral heritage to Broadway in a powerful, modern showcase. Headlining the evening is Alim Kamara, an internationally respected storyteller, author, and cultural educator from Sierra Leone, known for dynamic performances that blend folklore, history, humor, and life lessons for audiences of all ages. title: Move to the Rhythm
tagline: Close out April with creativity in motion!
description: At the Children’s Museum of Manhattan Apr 23–30, Move to the Rhythm blends jazz, movement, and hands-on art for families. Kids ages 0–6 can dance to live music, join a themed jazz party, draw and build simple sculptures with provided PlayWorks materials. Free with museum admission, it’s a playful way for little ones to explore sound, rhythm and making.

title: Macy’s Flower Show
tagline: Experience Homegrown—Celebrating America’s 250th Anniversary
description: Walk through Macy’s Herald Square (Apr 23–May 10) to see thousands of blooms, stained-glass garden panels and yarn-wrapped trees marking America’s 250th. Kids can hunt for different flowers, touch the yarn-wrapped trunks and get simple craft ideas from the displays. It’s free and family-friendly — an easy spring outing in NYC.
description_source: Macy’s Flower Show transforms Macy’s Herald Square into a springtime floral extravaganza featuring thousands of blooming plants, towering planters, stained-glass panels, and fiber art installations. The 2026 edition, themed 'Homegrown,' celebrates America’s 250th anniversary with immersive garden displays across main floors, balconies, and windows. Free and open to the public.

title: Toddies Act + Play (Toddies Crew)
tagline: A wonderful space to build community and learn performing arts techniques!
description: At The Studio on Main in East Hampton, Toddies Act + Play is a playful class for 4–5-year-olds mixing acting, dance, music and crafts. Kids play games, try simple scenes and songs, and do short performances to build imagination, turn-taking and confidence. Full session $45 or $20 drop-in.
description_source: The Toddies Crew is designed for children ages 4–5 who have not yet started kindergarten. Sessions include acting, dance, music training, and performance training alongside arts and crafts activities. Participants gain exposure to performing arts, teamwork, creativity, and self-expression. The program is offered as a full session or drop-in classes.

title: Rising NYRR Spring Jamboree Presented by TCS
tagline: Track and Field for Kids
description: Bring kids to Icahn Stadium on Randall's Island for a free, family-friendly track and field day for ages 2–18. Children can try age-appropriate sprints, relays, long jump, shot put, obstacle courses and distance runs, learning basic skills, teamwork and sportsmanship. Participants get a New Balance souvenir while supplies last.
description_source: Take the kids to Icahn Stadium on Randall’s Island for a fun day of track and field events! Kids can choose from age-appropriate activities they’d like to try, including sprints, a longer distance run, relays on the track, shot put, long jump, obstacle courses, and more! Registration is FREE and open to participants ages 2–18 of all abilities. Participants will receive a New Balance souvenir (while supplies last).

title: Long Island Tulip Festival at Waterdrinker
tagline: Experience a breathtaking celebration of spring with over 2.5 million tulips and family activities across two farms.
description: Walk through 2.5 million tulips at Waterdrinker Manorville, pick stems ($1 each) and enjoy greenhouses, food trucks, and live music. Kids (about ages 2–12) can feed barnyard animals, bounce on jumbo jump pads, play mini‑golf and explore a Dutch‑style Little Amsterdam while learning about flowers and farm life. Admission $20 weekdays, $25 weekends; under 2 free.
description_source: A spring festival featuring over 2.5 million blooming tulips across Waterdrinker’s Manorville and Riverhead farms. Includes mini-golf, petting zoos, live music, food trucks, photo opportunities, and a Dutch-inspired Little Amsterdam Walk-Thru. Tulip stems may be picked for $1 each. Open to all ages.
price_summary: $25 weekends; $20 weekdays; $1 per tulip stem for U-pick; children 2 and under free

title: Walk For Resilience 5K Run/Walk
tagline: A community-centered mental health event
description: This is a community 5K run/walk hosted by Carmel’s Depression & Anxiety Wellness Foundation to raise awareness and promote mental wellness. Families (ages 5–99) can walk, jog, or run at their own pace while kids get to move, hear a DJ, grab light snacks, and pose for free finish-line photos. Free to join at Prospect Park with timing, bibs, water, and live results.
description_source: The Walk for Resilience 5K Run/Walk is hosted by Carmel’s Depression & Anxiety Wellness Foundation (CDAWF). This inclusive 5K event brings together walkers, runners, families, and supporters to raise awareness for depression and anxiety while promoting movement, connection, and healing. Designed for all fitness levels, participants may walk, jog, or run at their own pace. The event emphasizes togetherness rather than competition, creating a supportive environment for mental health conversations.

title: Katy’s Courage 5K Run/Walk
tagline: Run/Walk for Local Scholarships and Cancer Research
description: A community 5K run/walk in Sag Harbor that raises money for local scholarships and cancer research through Katy’s Courage. Families and kids ages 5–100 can join — participants get a t-shirt, DJ music, hot chocolate, donuts and bagels, plus age-category awards for top finishers. Registration runs $25–$33.80.
description_source: Join us for the Katy’s Courage 5K Run/Walk in Sag Harbor, NY on April 18, 2026. The event starts at 8:30 AM, rain or shine. Proceeds from the event will go to Katy's Courage, supporting local scholarships and cancer research. Participants can enjoy amenities like t-shirts, music, and refreshments, along with awards for top finishers in various categories. Register now to secure your spot!
