title: It’s Our Only Earth Day 5K
tagline: Celebrate and bring awareness to preserving Mother Earth!
description: Family-friendly 5K through the tree-lined bike paths of Eisenhower Park (Field 2), wheelchair accessible and stroller friendly, starting at 9:00 AM. Kids ages 5–15 can run the full route or join the free Kiddie Dash at 8:30 AM to try a short, fun race and earn a T-shirt plus snacks from Waterloo Sparkling Water and All Round Foods. Registration starts at $33.80; proceeds support youth and special-needs running programs.
description_source: Join the It's Our Only Earth Day 5K and help raise awareness for environmental preservation. This exciting 5K will take place through the tree-lined bike paths in Eisenhower Park. Everyone is invited to participate and enjoy some tasty treats from All Round Foods and Waterloo Sparkling Water. Don't miss out on the FREE Kiddie Dash for younger participants at 8:30 AM!
price_summary: Registration prices vary based on options: base fee $33.80; add-ons include Virtual 5K ($35) and Bib mailing ($10). Free entry for Kiddie Dash.

description: Watch the International Public Policy Forum finals at NYU’s King Juan Carlos Center, where eight high‑school teams from 39 countries debate a G20 education tax motion. title: Fourteenth Annual Katy’s Courage 5k
tagline: Join us for a community run to support local scholarships!
description: Join the fourteenth annual Katy’s Courage 5K in Sag Harbor to help fund local scholarships. It’s a family-friendly community run where kids (ages 5–12) can try a real 5K, run with neighbors, and earn a finisher medal. Register $25 in advance or $30 day-of; check-in 7–8:15 a.m., start 8:30 a.m.
description_source: Katy’s Courage (P.O. Box 3251, Sag Harbor, NY 11963) is hosting the fourteenth annual Katy’s Courage 5K on Saturday, April 18, rain or shine. Net proceeds from this year’s race will support Katy’s Courage, a 501(c)(3) organization, with a portion of funds allocated to local scholarships. title: Pete the Cat
tagline: Join Jimmy and Pete on an adventure of friendship!
description: Catch TheaterWorksUSA's Pete the Cat at the Long Island Children’s Museum in Garden City — a lively musical about Pete and classmate Jimmy navigating second‑grade art class. Kids ages 3–8 can sing along to original songs, join playful storytelling, and see the characters work through creative blocks and friendship. Sensory‑friendly performances available; tickets about $9–$15.
description_source: Pete the Cat, the groovy blue cat from the beloved book series by Kimberly and James Dean, teams up with young Jimmy Biddle, a meticulous second grader struggling in art class. Together, they embark on a musical adventure to overcome creative blocks, learning about inspiration and friendship along the way. The performance features original music by Will Aronson and is presented by TheaterWorksUSA in collaboration with the Long Island Children’s Museum.

title: South Street Seaport Museum Lenape Games
tagline: Explore the traditions of Indigenous games!
description: At the South Street Seaport Museum families can try traditional Lenape games like Selahtinalìtin (pick-up-sticks style) and Mamantuhwin (dice-and-bowl). Kids ages 5–15 play hands-on, testing coordination and simple strategy while hearing short stories about Lenape life. It's family-friendly and museum-hosted, free to attend. Snacks provided.
description_source: Interactive games inspired by the Lenape people, including Selahtinalìtin (a scattering straws game similar to Pick Up Sticks) and Mamantuhwin (a dice-and-bowl game inspired by the whirligig beetle). title: Cumbe Center for African Dance
tagline: An interactive experience celebrating African rhythms
description: At the Staten Island Children’s Museum, Cumbe Center leads an interactive African dance and music session for kids ages 5–12 (free with admission). Children try traditional instruments, learn lively steps, and hear the stories behind the rhythms. It’s hands-on movement that builds rhythm, teamwork, and cultural curiosity.
description_source: Feel the joy, energy, and heartbeat of rhythms rooted in African culture! In this interactive experience, kids won’t just listen – they’ll move and groove. Young participants will explore traditional African instruments, discover the stories behind the beats, and learn vibrant dances that celebrate community, creativity, and connection. Get ready to clap, stomp, and dance your way through an unforgettable cultural adventure!

title: Sea Shanty Sessions
tagline: Join maritime music enthusiasts for a sing-along of authentic sea shanties in a historic setting.
description: Sing along to traditional maritime songs at the Noble Maritime Collection, led by the Folk Music Society of New York. Free, family-friendly sessions (third Sunday, 2–4 PM) honor sailor-song collector William Main Doerflinger. Kids can learn call-and-response shanties, try simple rhythms, and hear short stories about life at sea.
description_source: Led by the Folk Music Society of New York, this program offers a great opportunity to experience authentic, time-honored maritime songs in a historic setting. Sea Shanty Sessions are dedicated to William Main Doerflinger (1909-2000), the music scholar who collected traditional sailor’s songs from the residents of Sailor’s Snug Harbor. The sessions are free and family-friendly, and you are encouraged to sing along if the mood strikes you!

title: The Planter and the Plant – A Workshop Performance
tagline: A journey exploring the relationship between a planter and a plant.
description: A 30-minute theatre workshop-performance at QED Astoria for kids aged 3–8 and their families, free to attend. Children follow a planter and a seed as the plant grows, watching their relationship shift from curiosity to care and farewell. Short pre- and post-show activities let kids try ideas and share feedback while the piece is in development.
description_source: The Planter plants. Food, drinks, anything. One day, a seed grows into a plant. What follows is a journey exploring their evolving relationship: from getting to know the plant, to witnessing its unique growth, and finally, its departure. This is a theatre piece for 3–8 year olds and their families. This is a work-in-progress performance of a new theatre piece. We’re still in the exploration stage, and workshopping is the best way to gather audience feedback and discover what’s working, and what isn’t.

title: The Museum of Modern Art Presents Frida and Diego: The Last Dream
tagline: A first-of-its-kind collaboration with the Metropolitan Opera
description: At MoMA, this exhibition puts Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera side by side to show how they influenced each other. Kids ages 5–12 can look at bold, colorful paintings and try hands-on activities that teach simple art techniques, storytelling, and ways to express emotions. Free for visitors 16 and under; adults $17–$30.
description_source: Frida and Diego: The Last Dream celebrates Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera—two of Mexico’s most beloved 20th-century artists—in a collaboration with the Metropolitan Opera. The exhibition features artworks by Kahlo and Rivera in a setting designed by Jon Bausor, coinciding with the Met’s opera production El Último Sueño de Frida y Diego, composed by Gabriela Lena Frank with libretto by Nilo Cruz. The presentation includes six paintings and a drawing by Kahlo and multiple works by Rivera, highlighting their artistic relationship and enduring influence on visual and performing arts.
subway: E to 53rd St; 7 to 34th St-Hudson Yards
price_summary: Free for ages 16 and younger; $17–$30 for adults (admission required)

title: Face Value: Celebrity Press Photography
tagline: The first major exhibition of Hollywood studio portraiture from the Museum’s film stills archive
description: At MoMA, Face Value displays over 200 Hollywood studio portraits (1921–1996) that show how studios shaped celebrity images. Kids up to about 12 can hunt for faces like Louis Armstrong, Bette Davis and Elvis and see how photos were edited and styled to create glamour. It's free and a quick visit that sparks conversations about photos, fame, and how images are made.
description_source: Face Value examines how 20th-century Hollywood studios produced and manipulated photographic portraits to construct celebrity glamour for public consumption, prior to digital tools and social media. The exhibition features over 200 film stills from MoMA’s archive, highlighting editorial practices, stylized motifs, and the institutional machinery behind celebrity imagery.

title: The Orchid Show: Mr. Flower Fantastic’s Concrete Jungle
tagline: A vibrant celebration of New York City
description: Step into the Enid A. Haupt Conservatory at the New York Botanical Garden to see Mr. Flower Fantastic’s orchid-built take on the NYC skyline. Kids ages 3–12 can hunt for blooms, touch plants in hands-on programs, and learn about urban ecology and how orchids grow. Tickets $12–$25; members enter free with a reservation.
description_source: The Orchid Show: Mr. Flower Fantastic’s Concrete Jungle transforms the Enid A. Haupt Conservatory at the New York Botanical Garden into an immersive installation blending orchid artistry with New York City’s urban landscape. Features floral interpretations of skyscrapers, street culture, and pop art, created by designer Mr. Flower Fantastic. Includes guided tours and educational programming. Separate evening event (Orchid Nights) for adults 21+ with music and cocktails.

tagline: Kick off spring with Brooklyn’s creative community
description: Brooklyn Makes is a pop-up market at Empire Stores with 35+ local makers selling handmade jewelry, clothing, body care, home goods, tableware and artisanal foods. title: Sunday Science: Reptile Rumble
tagline: Explore the fascinating world of reptiles!
description: Bring kids to Sunday Science: Reptile Rumble at the Center for Science Teaching and Learning in Rockville Centre. Children ages 5–12 will meet and gently handle live reptiles like pythons and axolotls, try color-changing chameleon putty, and play camouflage games while learning about habitats and diets. Tickets $12–15.

This event is perfect for young budding scientists and reptile enthusiasts alike!

title: Rodgers & Hammerstein’s Cinderella
tagline: A timeless fairytale with a new spin
description: See Rodgers & Hammerstein’s Cinderella at the John W. Engeman Theater in Northport — a family-friendly musical with lively songs and dancing that retells Ella’s classic story. Best for kids about 5–15, it focuses on kindness, hope and following your own dreams while giving children bright visuals and tunes to clap along to. Tickets start at $15; a three-show family package is $45.
description_source: Rodgers & Hammerstein’s Cinderella follows Ella, a young woman who lives as a servant to her cruel stepmother and stepsisters after her father’s death. title: ASL Slam at Lincoln Center
tagline: Join the celebration of American Sign Language and the Deaf/signing community
description: ASL Slam is a live showcase of American Sign Language poetry and storytelling at Lincoln Center’s David Rubenstein Atrium, part of the Big Umbrella Festival. Teens (13–18) will watch traditional, contemporary and experimental signed pieces, meet performers from the Deaf/signing community, and see how ASL uses rhythm, space and facial expression to tell stories. Free entry.
description_source: Having just celebrated their 20th anniversary in 2025, ASL Slam joins the Big Umbrella Festival at the David Rubenstein Atrium this April. The company’s mission is to preserve, nurture, showcase, and promote American Sign Language and the Deaf/signing community through performance by offering a stage for community members to share and create traditional, contemporary, and experimental sign language literature. ASL Slam provides a safe space for the Sign Language community to play with their language and art.

title: MainStage Concert: The Places That Inspire Us
tagline: Music inspired by real-world settings
description: At Our Saviour’s Atonement, WHCO presents Anna Clyne’s Restless Oceans alongside Samuel Barber’s Knoxville: Summer of 1915, with soprano Ariadne Greif. The program explores music that paints sea turbulence and childhood memories, so expect vivid, narrative-driven pieces. Good for listeners who enjoy emotionally rich classical music. description_source: In The Places That Inspire Us, we explore music inspired by real-world settings and the emotional landscapes they evoke. title: Celebrate Holi!
tagline: Join us for the Festival of Colors
description: Take the kids to Long Island Children’s Museum in Garden City for Holi, a hands-on spring festival celebration. Children ages 3–10 can watch Srijan Dance Company perform Indian classical and folk dances, join craft stations like elephant decorating and Krishna masks, taste sweets and rosewater, and try flower-based gulal at 12:30 or 2:30. Free with museum admission.
description_source: Holi, a Hindu festival originating in India, marks the end of winter and the arrival of spring. Attendees throw colored powder (gulal), enjoy traditional folk music and dance performances by the Srijan Dance Company, participate in craft stations, and consume Holi-themed snacks and sweets. Color powder throwing sessions occur at 12:30 PM and 2:30 PM. Activities include elephant decoration, mask crafting inspired by Krishna, and rosewater drinks. All activities are included with general admission.

tagline: Story-time, crafts, and interactive activities for young transportation fans.

title: Steps Toward Healing 5K Run/Walk
tagline: No one should have to walk, or grieve, alone.
description: This is a 5K run/walk at Eisenhower Park raising funds for grief resources and support groups; families and kids ages 5+ are welcome. Kids can walk or run at their own pace and share in a supportive community; entry is $33.80.

Amenities include restrooms, free parking, swag bags, finish line photos, and live results.

title: Patterns at Lincoln Center
tagline: A multi-sensory dance performance for all ages
description: Patterns is a multi-sensory dance performance at Alice Tully Hall by AXIS Dance Company, with D/deaf, neurodiverse, disabled and non-disabled dancers sharing the stage. It's a relaxed, sensory-friendly show with a chill-out space and short interactive moments where kids (ages 5–18) can move along and try simple dance ideas. Pay-what-you-can (suggested $5).
description_source: AXIS Dance Company presents 'Patterns,' a dance performance featuring disabled, non-disabled, D/deaf, and neurodiverse performers. The event is designed as a Relaxed Performance, with sensory accommodations including a chill-out space and sensory materials, and is part of the Big Umbrella Festival for neurodiverse audiences. title: Family Concert: Music of Mozart
tagline: Explore the music of Mozart with your family!
description: Spend an afternoon at The Morgan with Young Concert Artists playing music by Mozart. Kids ages 5+ can listen, sing, and move along, then try hands-on artmaking and explore the Mozart exhibition. Tickets include museum admission; $30 adult/$10 child ($20 adult for members).
description_source: Visit the Morgan for an afternoon concert for our youngest visitors! This family-friendly performance with Young Concert Artists invites children to listen, sing, and move as they explore the music of Mozart. Pre- and post-concert activities include artmaking and a gallery exploration in the exhibition, Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart: Treasures from the Mozarteum Foundation of Salzburg. Recommended for families with children ages 5 and up. Families with children of all ages and abilities are welcome.

title: Two if by Sea: Lantern Workshop
tagline: Join us for a fun and illuminating craft workshop!
description: Join the Whaling Museum in Cold Spring Harbor on the anniversary of Paul Revere’s ride for a hands-on lantern workshop. Kids (ages 5–12) will hear how sailors used lanterns and how signals guided events during the Revolution, then make and decorate a decoupaged jar lantern. Suitable for young crafters; $8 members / $12 participants, and each child takes their lantern home.

title: Antarctica! Crew Wanted
tagline: Join our crew on a unique adventure across ice and water!
description: Antarctica! Crew Wanted is a sensory, dialogue-free theater adventure at Samuel Rehearsal Studio inspired by Shackleton’s 1915 voyage, made for neurodivergent kids ages 6–12. Through music, movement and hands-on sensory stations, children step into icy scenes, try teamwork tasks and explore basic survival ideas. Pay-what-you-can from $5; it’s calm, tactile, and lets kids lead the action.
description_source: An immersive sensory theater experience inspired by Ernest Shackleton’s 1915 Antarctic expedition, when his ship Endurance became trapped in ice. Created by Irish artists Phillida Eves and Amélie Bal, the performance uses music, movement, and environmental design to guide neurodivergent audiences ages 6–12 through an interactive journey of survival, teamwork, and exploration. No spoken dialogue; sensory cues and physical storytelling drive the narrative.

title: Downtown Brooklyn Car-Free Earth Day 2026
tagline: Join an afternoon of Earth-friendly and car-free activities
description: Spend an afternoon car-free at Albee Square with free family activities for kids 5–14. Children can learn to ride confidently with Bike New York, join soccer sessions with Brooklyn FC, try hand-sewing with Black Girls Sew and make art from reused materials; there's also e-waste drop-off and DJ music. Free.

Albee Square West will be closed to traffic, so bring your bikes or roller skates, and enjoy activities and music at Albee Square. Here’s what’s happening: On Albee Square West 12:00 PM – 3:00 PM In partnership with Elian Electronics Recycling, you are invited to dispose of your e-waste in a free, convenient, and ethical way. 1:00 PM – 3:00 PM Kick-off spring with action-packed soccer fun sponsored by Brooklyn FC, Brooklyn’s professional soccer team. Bike lessons by Bike New York for kids aged 5-14. Hand sewing with Black Girls Sew. Art projects from reused materials.

title: Earth Day How-To Festival
tagline: Join us as we transform the museum into a giant classroom celebrating community science!
description: At the Staten Island Museum, the Earth Day How‑To Festival fills the galleries with hands-on demos, short talks, and drop-in workshops about community science. Kids ages 5–12 can try simple experiments, citizen‑science projects, bike basics and habitat monitoring with help from local volunteers. Free with museum admission — a practical, active way for families to learn about the environment.
description_source: Hands-on workshops, drop-in presentations, and scheduled lectures throughout the Staten Island Museum focus on community science. Local experts, enthusiasts, and professionals demonstrate practical skills ranging from bike repair to origami, encouraging families to learn and engage with environmental stewardship.

title: ARF’s Kitten Shower
tagline: The CUTEST baby shower of the year… is for kittens!
description: Bring kids to ARF’s Kitten Shower at the ARF Adoption Center in East Hampton for a hands‑on look at caring for newborn kittens. description_source: Join ARF Hamptons for an annual event at the ARF Adoption Center on April 11, 2026, from 12:00 PM to 2:00 PM to support orphaned kittens during kitten season. The event supports kittens requiring round-the-clock care and promotes adoption and volunteer opportunities.

title: Jackie Robinson Day Weekend
tagline: Celebrate Jackie Robinson's legacy
description: Celebrate Jackie Robinson at the Jackie Robinson Museum with family-friendly activities; members get free entry (non-members $15–$18, under 5 free). Kids ages 4–15 can join story time, crafts, cap customization, a scavenger hunt, a life-size game board and youth financial workshops. Children must be accompanied by an adult. Events: April 11, 11:30am–3pm, and April 15, 11am–4pm.
description_source: Commemorate Jackie Robinson’s 1947 debut with the Brooklyn Dodgers, breaking baseball’s color barrier. Activities include story time and crafts for children ages 4–6, baseball cap customization, interactive scavenger hunts, a life-size game board, financial empowerment workshops for youth 10–15, and photo stations. Museum admission required. Events run 11:30am–3pm on April 11, 2026. Additional community celebration on April 15, 2026, 11am–4pm, open to youth baseball and softball players.
price_summary: Free with museum admission; $15–$18 admission fee applies for non-members. Children under 5 free.

title: ASL Baby Slam at Lincoln Center
tagline: A family-friendly showcase promoting playful language acquisition through sign language.
description: ASL Baby Slam at the David Rubenstein Atrium is a free, family-friendly performance of American Sign Language as part of the Big Umbrella Festival. Kids can watch signed stories and join interactive language play to try simple signs, rhythm, and gestures. It's suited for babies, toddlers and caregivers who want a welcoming way to explore ASL and meet the Deaf community.
description_source: Having just celebrated their 20th anniversary in 2025, ASL Slam joins the Big Umbrella Festival at the David Rubenstein Atrium this April. The company’s mission is to preserve, nurture, showcase, and promote American Sign Language and the Deaf/signing community through performance by offering a stage for community members to share and create traditional, contemporary, and experimental sign language literature. ASL Slam provides a safe space for the Sign Language community to play with their lang...

title: Big Umbrella Festival at Lincoln Center
tagline: A multi-week series of performances and installations designed for the neurodiverse community
description: Big Umbrella Festival at Lincoln Center runs for several weeks with sensory‑friendly performances, workshops and outdoor installations made for neurodiverse kids and families. Kids and teens (about ages 2–18) can try movement, music, theater, comedy and hands‑on art in calm spaces with staff support and chill‑out areas. Most events are free or pay‑what‑you‑can.
description_source: The Big Umbrella Festival is a multi-week series of free and choose-what-you-pay performances, workshops, and outdoor installations at Lincoln Center, designed with and for neurodiverse audiences. Events include immersive theater, music workshops, ASL Slam performances, live performances, interactive installations, and digital content across dance, music, theater, comedy, and art. All events are accessible and welcoming to audiences of all ages, with specific programming for children, teens, and young adults.

title: The Mary Ruchalski Foundation 5K & Fun Run
tagline: Raising funds for pediatric cancer research and supporting families affected by pediatric cancer
description: Run or walk a 5K at Eisenhower Park and stick around for the family Fun Run—check‑in 8 AM, 5K at 9 AM. Kids (ages 5–18) get a personalized bib and T‑shirt, finish photos, bottled water and the simple thrill of racing with friends; there are live results, raffles and prize drawings. Entry starts at $18.28; proceeds support pediatric cancer research.
description_source: Join us for the Mary Ruchalski Foundation 5K & Fun Run on Saturday, April 11, 2026, at Eisenhower Park. title: Watson Adventures’ Wizard School Scavenger Hunt
tagline: A magical art scavenger hunt!
description: Spend two hours at the Met on a scavenger hunt that pairs artworks with Harry Potter–style characters and themes. Kids 10–15 (must attend with an adult) will hunt for visual clues, practice spotting artistic details, and link familiar book moments to real paintings and sculptures; adult-only teams welcome. Museum admission and fees ($35–$45) are included.
description_source: Teams explore the Metropolitan Museum of Art to identify artworks inspired by characters, creatures, and objects from the Harry Potter series, including sorceresses, Snape-like figures, owls, dragons, giants, centaurs, unicorns, mermaids, and sharks. tagline: Join CRESLI scientists on a 2-hour expedition to view Atlantic harbor seals in Shinnecock Bay.
description: Spend two hours aboard the R/V Peconic with CRESLI scientists exploring Shinnecock Bay to spot and photograph Atlantic harbor seals. title: 50 Years of Apple Computers: The Kevin Lenane Collection
tagline: Celebrating half a century of innovation
description: See how Apple machines changed computing at the Long Island Museum in Kevin Lenane’s restored collection, including a rare 1976 Apple‑1 and early Lisa and Mac artifacts. Kids (recommended ages 6–12) can spot old keyboards, compare chunky designs to today’s tablets, and learn how personal computers evolved. Free with museum admission (adults $15; kids under 6 free).
description_source: Exhibition featuring the most complete collection of Apple computers in the United States, assembled and restored by Long Island resident Kevin Lenane. Includes a rare 1976 Apple-1 computer, artifacts from the Lisa and Mac development projects of the 1980s, and 1990s experiments with personal digital assistants and televisions.

title: Video Games: The Great Connector
tagline: Explore the role of video games in youth development
description: At the Long Island Museum, this free exhibition has 10 hands-on gaming stations where kids (ages 5–17) can play cooperative and puzzle games. Children can try basic game design, hear stories of Black and Latinx creators, and see Long Island history like 1959’s Tennis for Two. It’s a playful way to practice teamwork and problem-solving; registration is needed for the curator tour.
description_source: An exhibition featuring 10 interactive gaming stations that highlight video games as tools for developing teamwork, problem-solving, and social skills. It includes historical context on Long Island’s contributions to gaming, such as 'Tennis for Two' (1959), and showcases the work of Black and Latinx designers. Visitors engage with classic and modern games while learning about careers in game development.

price_summary: Free admission for all visitors; registration required for curator tour.

title: Faith Ringgold: Artist, Storyteller, Activist
tagline: Exploring diversity through literacy and storytelling.
description: This exhibition at the Sugar Hill Children's Museum displays Faith Ringgold’s paintings, illustrated scenes from MLK Jr.’s Letter from Birmingham Jail, working drawings and children’s-book manuscripts. Kids ages 5–12 can explore storytelling and social justice through bold images and story-quilt ideas, seeing how an artist turns thoughts into books. Free admission makes it easy to bring the family.
description_source: Artist, writer, educator and activist Faith Ringgold was born in Harlem, not far from the Sugar Hill Museum. It was here that she had her first art studio, became an art teacher and began to raise her own family. Her love for the children of Harlem inspired Broadway Housing Communities to create the museum. The exhibition includes eight illustrated scenes from Martin Luther King, Jr.’s Letter From Birmingham City Jail, along with facsimiles of her working drawings and digital reproductions of ma... title: Basketball Clinic at Southampton Youth Services (SYS)
tagline: Join our basketball clinic for grades 7-12!
description: This basketball clinic for grades 7–12 meets Fridays, 5–7pm from Jan 16–May 15 (13 sessions; no class Feb 20, Mar 27, Apr 3 & 10, May 1) at Southampton Youth Services. Coaches run drills on shooting, ball-handling and team play so teens (ages 12–18) get skill practice and game-style experience. $50 covers the full series; space is limited, so pre-register.
description_source: The Town of Southampton Youth Bureau will be sponsoring a Basketball Clinic at Southampton Youth Services (SYS), 1370a Majors Path every Friday from 5:00pm – 7:00pm (January 16th, 2026 – May 15th, 2026). Program will not be held on February 20th, March 27th, April 3rd, April 10th, and May 1st. Clinic is open to grades 7 – 12 and the cost is $50 for all 13 sessions. Space is limited and pre-registration is required! For more information or to register online, please call (631) 702-2425 or visit http://www.southamptontownny.gov/YBactivities.

title: CAMP New York City
tagline: Immersive, themed play experiences for kids in the heart of NYC
description: CAMP NYC is an indoor play space in Manhattan with themed rooms like Bluey’s House, a Slime Bar, and hands-on craft stations for kids ages 3–10. Children can make slime, build crafts, and role-play in immersive sets, practicing creativity and fine motor skills while taking projects home. Admission is $30–$45; the venue is stroller- and wheelchair-accessible.
description_source: CAMP New York City provides interactive, themed play experiences for children aged 3–10, featuring immersive Bluey environments, messy slime-making, and creative craft stations. The venue is stroller and wheelchair accessible, with no advanced registration required for general admission. Additional paid experiences may be available.
subway: 14th St - Union Square (4, 5, 6, N, Q, R, L lines)
price_summary: Admission ranges from $30–$45; includes access to all core activities. Some premium experiences may cost extra.

title: Paint Your heART Out! Painting Class for Kids
tagline: Explore color and imagination through painting
description: A 60-minute painting class at The Art Studio NY for kids ages 6–10 that explores acrylic and watercolor techniques. Kids try layering, blending and composition while making several pieces inspired by famous artists and hearing fun art-history tidbits. All materials are provided, and the session focuses on play and skill-building. Free class (sold out).
description_source: In this class, young artists will discover the joy of painting through personalized instruction, learning techniques like layering, blending, and composition. They'll engage with art history fun-facts and create their own unique artworks inspired by famous artists. The class encourages self-expression and enhances skills such as focus and communication. All materials are included, making for a comprehensive and enjoyable learning experience.

title: RECESS!! Epic Interactive Family Fun at a Surreal Playground!
tagline: Imagine Willy Wonka created a surreal playground with 20+ experiences!
description: RECESS!! is an indoor interactive playground at Empire Outlets with 20+ hands-on installations inspired by a surreal, Willy Wonka–style setting. Suited for ages 2–10, kids will climb, tinker with objects, and try sensory and imaginative play that encourages creativity and cooperative games. Parents can join in or watch from nearby. Tickets $18–$28.
description_source: Recess!! is an extraordinary interactive playground experience that promises fun for the whole family. Each visit allows you to explore a variety of whimsical and engaging installations that inspire creativity and play. Don't miss the opportunity to visit this unique playground before it ends!
subway: Staten Island Ferry Terminal (SIR) at St. George, then 5-minute walk to Empire Outlets
